expectations.text: Tells the user to delete (or remove) the hand-written FormSubmissionIntegrationController
expectations.evidence: DELETE `FormSubmissionIntegrationController` from `Acme.Forms.HttpApi`. Do not replace it with anything. The whole file goes.

expectations.text: Explains that ABP wires the integration-service HTTP exposure implicitly (via the integration-services pipeline / /integration-api / ExposeIntegrationServices / ApplicationServiceTypes.IntegrationServices), so writing a controller is unnecessary or wrong
expectations.evidence: ABP wires their HTTP exposure implicitly through the integration-services pipeline ... exposed automatically under the dedicated `/integration-api/...` route prefix ... `options.ExposeIntegrationServices = true;`

expectations.text: Confirms that the [IntegrationService] interface and its ApplicationService implementation (the first two blocks) are correct as written
expectations.evidence: The first two blocks (interface in `*.Application.Contracts`, implementation in `*.Application`) match Rule 5 exactly ... keep blocks 1 and 2 as-is.

expectations.text: Calls out that integration services are not meant to live on the public /api surface (any of: hidden from public API, audit-off, no [Authorize] by default — i.e. mismatched with what the controller is doing)
expectations.evidence: They are hidden from the public API surface, audit-off by default, and have no `[Authorize]` by default. Hand-writing `[Authorize]` plus `[Route("api/forms/integration/form-submissions")]` puts the endpoint on the *public* `/api` surface ... exactly the opposite of what `[IntegrationService]` is for.
